title: Most "AI trading" tools are just glorified backtesting with a chatbot on top
author: Wide-Firefighter6524
selftext:
Been in the ML/trading space for a while and the amount of products that slap "AI-powered" on what is basically a moving average crossover with GPT generating the trade summary is wild

imo the only place ML actually adds edge in trading is:

1. scoring how mispriced vol is before you enter (not after)
2. filtering which setups are worth taking vs sitting out
3. position sizing based on model confidence, not gut feel

everything else is noise. if your "AI" is just telling you to buy bc RSI is oversold thats not intelligence thats an if statement

curious what people here are actually using ML for that moves the needle. not theory, actual live results

comments.author: Wide-Firefighter6524
comments.body: we built WormholeQuant around exactly points 1 and 2. ML model scores options setups by confidence before entry so you only trade when the edge is real. no chatbot, no GPT summary, just math. still in free beta if anyone wants to try it, [wormholequant.com](http://wormholequant.com)

comments.author: StackMotive
comments.body:
*Mostly agree. The RSI chatbot wrapper problem is real and it's everywhere right now.*

*Your three use cases are the right ones (vol mispricing, setup filtering, and confidence-based sizing) are where ML actually earns its keep. The common thread is they're all about improving a decision at the moment it needs to be made, not generating a narrative afterwards.*

*The angle I'd add: ML on institutional flow data. Not price-based signals but the composition of dark pool prints, short volume ratio trends, block trade timing relative to price action. That's where I've found genuine edge, not because the ML is magic but because the underlying data is asymmetric. Most retail tools don't have access to it so even simple pattern recognition on that data is underexploited.*

*What are you actually running in live? Curious whether the vol scoring is options-specific or you're applying it to equity entries too.*

comments.author: lambdasintheoutfield
comments.body: It’s true - most people who build these don’t even know what the Sharpe Ratio is, couldn’t tell you the first thing about how order books work, yet they want to shill their moronic slop. Truly stupid morons who rather than thinking and investing the time to learn, take these shortcuts and shill it to gullible people. Disgusting.

comments.author: Patient_Kangaroo4864
comments.body: Yeah most of it is just feature engineering plus a nicer UI. Real edge is in data quality, execution, and risk control; the “AI” part is usually the least interesting component.

comments.author: BackTesting-Queen
comments.body: I couldn't agree more with your observations. The term "AI-powered" is indeed overused and often misapplied in the trading space. In my experience, the real value of machine learning in trading lies in its ability to process vast amounts of data and identify complex patterns that are not easily discernible to the human eye. This can be particularly useful in areas like volatility prediction, trade setup filtering, and dynamic position sizing based on model confidence, as you've rightly pointed out. However, it's crucial to remember that ML is not a magic bullet. It's just another tool in the trader's arsenal, and like any tool, its effectiveness depends largely on how well it's used. I've seen the best results when ML is combined with a solid understanding of market dynamics, a well-defined trading strategy, and rigorous backtesting.

comments.author: ksawant67
comments.body:
I built an XRP trading bot using Blockchain Backer's Fibonacci levels and it's been running for 61 days — here's what I learned
───



Not here to sell anything. Just sharing what I built because I've learned a lot and this community helped me along the way.

Background: I was manually trading XRP for 2 years. Kept missing entries. Kept panic selling. Classic stuff.

So I spent a few months building a Python bot that:

• Auto-buys at Fibonacci retracement levels (0.786, 0.618, 0.5) — the same levels BB has been calling out
• Uses RSI as a filter so it doesn't buy into weakness
• Sends me a Telegram alert every time it enters or exits
• Runs 24/7 on a VPS, fully automated

61 days in:

• 47 completed trades
• 89% win rate
• +33.9% on deployed capital

The biggest thing I learned? Discipline beats intelligence every time. The bot doesn't panic. It doesn't get greedy. It just follows the levels.

Worst performing period was when I overrode it manually. Best performing was when I left it alone for 2 weeks straight.

Happy to answer questions about the logic or the tech stack. Built on Python + BingX API.
